info.title: Messaging Settings API
info.description:
The Messaging Settings API provides comprehensive management of email configurations for epilot organizations.

## Overview

This API enables organizations to:
- **Email Addresses**: Configure sender email addresses, set primary addresses, and manage email aliases
- **Email Signatures**: Create and manage HTML email signatures that can be associated with email addresses
- **Custom Domains**: Add and verify custom email domains for professional branding
- **Shared Inboxes**: Organize incoming emails into categorized inboxes with team assignments
- **Microsoft 365 Integration**: Connect Outlook mailboxes for seamless email synchronization

## Key Concepts

### Email Addresses
Email addresses represent the sender identities used when composing emails. Each address can have:
- A display name (e.g., "Sales Team")
- Assigned users and groups who can send from this address
- A default signature
- Association with a shared inbox

### Shared Inboxes
Shared inboxes help teams organize and manage incoming emails. Features include:
- Color-coded categorization
- Team member assignments
- Routing rules for incoming messages

### Email Signatures
HTML signatures that are automatically appended to outgoing emails. Signatures can include:
- Rich text formatting
- Images and logos
- Contact information

### Custom Domains
Organizations can configure custom email domains to send emails from their own domain (e.g., `support@yourcompany.com`). This requires:
1. Adding the domain
2. Configuring DNS records
3. Verifying domain ownership

## Authentication

All endpoints require authentication via Bearer token (JWT) unless otherwise specified.
Use the `x-epilot-org-id` header to specify the target organization when using shared tenant access.

tags.name: Email settings
tags.description:
Legacy settings endpoints for managing various email configuration types including signatures,
custom domains, email addresses, and duplicate detection settings.

tags.name: Email addresses
tags.description:
Manage email sender addresses for your organization. Email addresses define the identities
from which emails can be sent. Each address can be configured with display names, user/group
assignments, default signatures, and shared inbox associations.

tags.name: Shared inboxes
tags.description:
Shared inboxes provide team-based email organization. They allow multiple users to collaborate
on incoming messages with features like color-coding, assignee management, and email routing.
A default inbox is always available and does not need to be explicitly created.

tags.name: Inbox buckets
tags.description:
Inbox buckets are internal storage containers associated with shared inboxes. Each shared inbox
has a corresponding bucket for message storage. The default bucket is always available.

tags.name: O365 Outlook Connection
tags.description:
Microsoft Office 365 Outlook integration endpoints. These endpoints enable organizations to:
- Initiate OAuth connections with Microsoft 365
- Connect Outlook shared mailboxes
- Manage connection status and tokens
- Disconnect integrations when needed

The integration supports multiple Azure AD tenants and handles token refresh automatically.

tags.name: Settings
tags.description:
Generic settings management for various email configuration types:
- **signature**: HTML email signatures
- **email_domain**: Custom email domains
- **email_address**: Sender email addresses
- **whitelist_email_address**: Addresses exempt from duplicate detection
- **restrict_duplicates_within**: Time window for duplicate email detection

tags.name: Domains
tags.description:
Custom email domain management. Add, verify, and configure custom domains to send emails
from your organization's domain. Domain verification requires proper DNS configuration
including MX records and SPF/DKIM settings.

put.description:
Provisions or reactivates an epilot-managed email address for the organization.

When provisioning a new epilot email address, any previously active epilot email addresses
will be automatically deactivated. Only one epilot email address can be active at a time.

Epilot email addresses use the `@epilot.cloud` domain and are fully managed by the platform.

requestBody.description: The epilot email address to provision

post.description:
Sets the specified email address as the primary address for the organization.

The primary email address is used as the default sender address when composing new emails.
Only one email address can be primary at a time; setting a new primary will unset the previous one.

requestBody.description: The email address to set as primary

get.description:
Retrieves the details of a specific email address by its ID.

Returns the full configuration including display name, assigned users/groups,
default signature, and shared inbox association.

delete.description:
Permanently deletes an email address from the organization.

**Warning**: This action cannot be undone. Users will no longer be able to send
emails from this address after deletion.

put.description:
Updates the configuration of an existing email address.

You can modify:
- Display name
- Assigned users and groups
- Default signature
- Shared inbox association
- Active status

requestBody.description: The fields to update on the email address

get.description:
Retrieves all email addresses configured for the organization.

Returns an array of email address configurations including their IDs, display names,
assigned users/groups, signatures, and status flags.

post.description:
Adds a new email address to the organization.

The email address can be from a custom domain (if configured) or any external
email provider. Optionally assign users, groups, and a default signature.

requestBody.description: The email address configuration to create

get.description:
Retrieves the details of a specific shared inbox by its ID.

Returns the inbox configuration including name, color, description, assigned team members,
and the associated bucket ID.

delete.description:
Deletes a shared inbox and reroutes all associated emails to a successor inbox.

When a shared inbox is deleted:
- All email addresses associated with this inbox will be reassigned to the successor
- If no successor is specified, emails are routed to the default inbox
- The inbox's message history is preserved in the successor inbox

**Note**: The default inbox cannot be deleted.

parameters.name: successorInboxId
parameters.description:
ID of the inbox that will receive emails from the deleted inbox.
If not provided, the default inbox (`default`) will be used as the successor.

put.description:
Updates the configuration of an existing shared inbox.

You can modify the inbox name, color, description, and team member assignments.
Changes take effect immediately for all associated email addresses.

requestBody.description: The fields to update on the shared inbox

get.description:
Retrieves all shared inboxes configured for the organization.

**Note**: The default inbox (with ID `default`) is not included in this list but is
always available for all organizations. You do not need to create it explicitly.

post.description:
Creates a new shared inbox for the organization.

Shared inboxes help teams organize and categorize incoming emails.
Each inbox requires a name and color for visual identification.

requestBody.description: The shared inbox configuration to create

get.description:
Retrieves all inbox buckets for the organization.

Inbox buckets are internal storage containers that correspond to shared inboxes.
Each shared inbox has an associated bucket for storing messages.

**Note**: The default bucket (with ID `default`) is not included in this list but
is always available for all organizations.
Returns Microsoft authorization URL for Outlook OAuth.

Returns all Microsoft 365 / Outlook connections for the organization.
Supports multiple connections (one per Azure AD tenant).

Each connection includes an `action` field that tells the UI what button to show
and what endpoint to call. All actions use GET /outlook/connect.

connections.description: List of Outlook connections (one per tenant)

post.description:
Removes the Microsoft 365 / Outlook connection for a specific tenant.
This deletes the stored tokens and disconnects the integration.

tenant_id.description: Azure AD Tenant ID of the connection to disconnect
The tenant ID that was disconnected

affected_shared_inboxes.description: List of shared inbox IDs that were affected by the disconnection

400.description: Bad request - missing tenant_id or organization ID

post.description:
Connects an Outlook mailbox:
  1. Validates the user has access to the mailbox via Microsoft Graph API
  2. Creates a mapping between the email address of the mailbox and the outlook connection
  3. Enables the user to send emails as the mailbox's email address

email.description: Email address of the Outlook mailbox to connect

shared_inbox_id.description: Shared inbox ID to associate with the mailbox. Defaults to the default shared inbox.

mailboxSyncTimeframe.description:
Optional timeframe for initial mailbox sync. When provided, triggers an automatic
mailbox sync after connecting the mailbox, syncing emails from the specified period.

outlook_email.description: The email of the connected mailbox

tenant_id.description: Azure AD Tenant ID that provisions this mailbox

403.description: Access denied - user does not have access to the mailbox

409.description: A mailbox email already connected to an Outlook connection

post.description:
Disconnects a single Outlook mailbox by email address.
Deletes the email address entity, Outlook email mapping, and Graph API subscriptions.
Does not affect the tenant-level Outlook connection.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the Outlook mailbox to disconnect

email.description: The email address that was disconnected

404.description: No Outlook mapping found for this email address

post.description:
Triggers an Outlook mailbox sync for the specified email address.
Syncs existing emails (inbox + sent items) for the specified timeframe.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the Outlook mailbox to sync

timeframe.description:
Sync period:
- 5m: last 5 minutes (quick sync)
- 1w: 1 week
- 2w: 2 weeks
- 1m: 1 month

404.description: Mailbox not connected for the provided email address

409.description: A sync in progress for the provided mailbox

get.description:
Returns the current or latest sync status for the specified mailbox.
Poll this endpoint to track sync progress.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the Outlook mailbox

404.description: No sync found for this mailbox

post.description:
Retries failed messages from a previous sync execution.
Only retries messages with status FAILED (not PERMANENTLY_FAILED).
Messages that fail 3+ retries become PERMANENTLY_FAILED.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address of the Outlook mailbox

sync_id.description: Execution ID of the sync to retry

scope.description: Retry scope. Use 'all_failed' to retry all retryable failed messages.

message_ids.description: Specific Graph message IDs to retry (alternative to scope)

retry_execution_id.description: Execution ID for the retry sync

messages_queued.description: Number of messages queued for retry

404.description: No sync found or no retryable messages

409.description: A sync is already running for this mailbox

get.description: Returns all Outlook email addresses connected to the organization.
Exchanges authorization code for tokens and stores them.

Retrieves settings of a specific type for the organization.

If an `id` is provided, returns only that specific setting.
Otherwise, returns all settings of the specified type.

## Setting Types

| Type | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `signature` | HTML email signatures |
| `email_domain` | Custom email domains |
| `email_address` | Sender email addresses |
| `whitelist_email_address` | Addresses exempt from duplicate detection |
| `restrict_duplicates_within` | Time window for duplicate email detection |

parameters.name: type
parameters.description:
The type of settings to retrieve. Determines which category of
email settings will be returned.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
Optional ID to retrieve a specific setting. If omitted, all settings
of the specified type are returned.

200.description:
Successfully retrieved settings. Returns either a single setting object
(when `id` is provided) or an array of settings.

post.description:
Creates a new setting of the specified type.

The setting type determines which fields are applicable:
- **signature**: Requires `name`, `value` (plain text), and `html` (HTML content)
- **email_domain**: Requires `value` (domain name)
- **whitelist_email_address**: Requires `value` (email address)
- **restrict_duplicates_within**: Requires `value` (time duration like "5m", "1d")

create_whitelist.summary: Whitelist an email address from duplicate detection

delete.description:
Deletes a setting by its ID and type.

Both the `id` and `type` are required to uniquely identify the setting to delete.

**Warning**: This action cannot be undone.

id.description: The unique identifier of the setting to delete

200.description: Setting deleted successfully. Returns the deleted setting.

post.description:
Updates an existing setting identified by its ID.

Include the `type` field in the request body to specify which setting type
is being updated. Only the fields provided will be updated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the setting to update

200.description: Setting updated successfully. Returns the updated setting.

post.description:
Adds a custom email domain to the organization.

After adding the domain, you must:
1. Configure the required DNS records (provided in the response)
2. Verify the domain using the verification endpoint

Until verification is complete, the domain cannot be used for sending emails.

200.description:
Domain added successfully. The response includes DNS records that must be
configured before verification.

delete.description:
Removes a custom email domain from the organization.

**Warning**: Deleting a domain will prevent sending emails from any addresses
using this domain. Existing email addresses on this domain should be removed
or reassigned before deleting the domain.

post.description:
Verifies that the domain's name server (NS) records are correctly configured.

This check ensures that DNS resolution is properly set up for the domain
before proceeding with full domain verification.

Run this verification after configuring NS records in your DNS provider.

requestBody.description: The domain to verify name servers for

200.description:
Name server verification result. Check the response to see if
verification passed or if additional DNS configuration is needed.

post.description:
Verifies ownership and configuration of a custom email domain.

Domain verification checks:
- DNS TXT records for domain ownership
- MX records for email routing
- SPF records for sender authentication
- DKIM records for email signing

Once verified, the domain can be used to create email addresses and send emails.

200.description:
Domain verification result. The response indicates whether verification
passed and which DNS records (if any) still need configuration.

BadRequest.description:
The request was malformed or contains invalid data.
Check the error message for details on which fields are invalid.

Forbidden.description:
Access denied. The authenticated user does not have permission to perform
this operation. This may occur if:
- The user lacks the required role or permissions
- The resource belongs to a different organization

example.error: Insufficient permissions to access this resource

NotFound.description:
The requested resource was not found. Verify that:
- The resource ID is correct
- The resource exists in your organization
- The resource has not been deleted

Conflict.description:
The request conflicts with the current state of the resource.
Common causes include:
- Attempting to create a resource that already exists
- Concurrent modification conflicts
- Business rule violations (e.g., duplicate email address)

example.error: Email address already exists in another configuration

InternalServerError.description:
An unexpected error occurred on the server. If this error persists,
please contact support with the request details and timestamp.

OutlookErrorResponse.description:
An error response from the Outlook OAuth flow.
May include an admin consent URL if admin permissions are required.

EmailAddressId.name: id
EmailAddressId.description:
Unique identifier of the email address. This can be either the UUID assigned
when the email address was created, or the email address string itself.

SharedInboxId.name: id
SharedInboxId.description:
Unique identifier (UUID) of the shared inbox.
Use `default` to reference the organization's default inbox.

EpilotAuth.description:
Primary authentication using epilot OAuth2 bearer token.
Include the JWT token in the Authorization header: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

AsOrganization.name: x-ivy-org-id
AsOrganization.description:
**Internal use only.** Sets the organization context when using internal service-to-service
authentication. Not intended for external API consumers.

EpilotOrg.name: x-epilot-org-id
EpilotOrg.description:
Overrides the target organization for shared tenant access.
Use this header when you have access to multiple organizations and need to
specify which organization's data to access.

InboxBucketResponse.description: Inbox bucket representing the storage container for a shared inbox.

inbox_id.description: ID of the shared inbox associated with this bucket

ProvisionEpilotEmailAddressPayload.description: Request payload for provisioning an epilot-managed email address.

address.description: The epilot email address to provision (must be on @epilot.cloud domain)

SetEmailAddressPrimaryPayload.description: Request payload for setting an email address as the organization's primary address.

address.description: The email address to set as primary

UpdateEmailAddressPayload.description:
Request payload for updating an email address configuration.
All fields are optional; only provided fields will be updated.

name.description: Display name shown as the sender name in emails

user_ids.description: List of user IDs who can send from this address

group_ids.description: List of group IDs whose members can send from this address

default_signature_id.description: ID of the signature to use by default when sending from this address

shared_inbox_id.description: ID of the shared inbox to associate with this address

is_active.description: Whether the email address is active and can be used for sending

CreateEmailAddressPayload.description: Request payload for creating a new email address.

address.description: The email address to add (e.g., from a custom domain or external provider)

name.description: Display name shown as the sender name in emails

user_ids.description: List of user IDs who can send from this address

group_ids.description: List of group IDs whose members can send from this address

default_signature_id.description: ID of the signature to use by default when sending from this address

shared_inbox_id.description: ID of the shared inbox to associate with this address

EmailAddressResponse.description: Email address configuration with all associated metadata.

name.description: Display name shown as the sender name

user_ids.description: IDs of users who can send from this address

group_ids.description: IDs of groups whose members can send from this address

default_signature_id.description: ID of the default signature for this address

shared_inbox_id.description: ID of the associated shared inbox

is_active.description: Whether the address is currently active for sending

is_primary.description: Whether this is the organization's primary email address

is_epilot_email_address.description: Whether this is an epilot-managed address (@epilot.cloud)

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response format for all API errors.

error.description: Human-readable error message describing what went wrong

status.description: HTTP status code of the error

SettingMeta.description: Common metadata fields for all settings and resources.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the resource was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the resource was last updated

created_by.description: User ID of the user who created the resource

updated_by.description: User ID of the user who last updated the resource

UpdateSharedInboxPayload.description:
Request payload for updating a shared inbox configuration.
All fields are optional; only provided fields will be updated.

color.description: Hex color code for visual identification in the UI

name.description: Display name of the shared inbox

assignees.description: List of user IDs assigned to this inbox

description.description: Optional description of the inbox purpose

CreateSharedInboxPayload.description: Request payload for creating a new shared inbox.

id.description: Optional custom ID for the inbox (auto-generated if not provided)

color.description: Hex color code for visual identification in the UI (required)

name.description: Display name of the shared inbox (required)

assignees.description: List of user IDs to assign to this inbox

description.description: Optional description of the inbox purpose

SharedInboxResponse.description: Shared inbox configuration with all associated metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier of the shared inbox

name.description: Display name of the shared inbox

color.description: Hex color code for visual identification

assignees.description: List of user IDs assigned to this inbox

bucket_id.description: ID of the associated storage bucket for messages

ConnectedOutlookEmail.description:
Mapping between an Outlook email and its Outlook Connection.
This tracks which provider/tenant provisions each Outlook email.

outlook_email.description: The Outlook shared mailbox email address

tenant_id.description: Azure AD Tenant ID that provisions this mailbox

error.description: Error code or message from the OAuth flow.

admin_consent_url.description: URL for tenant admin to grant consent, if applicable.

status.description:
Current connection status:
- pending_auth: Admin consent granted, waiting for user OAuth
- connected: Fully connected with valid tokens
- expired: Tokens expired, need to re-authenticate
- not_connected: No connection, initiate OAuth

action.description:
Action for UI to take (all call GET /outlook/connect):
- connect: No connection, initiate OAuth
- authorize: Admin consent done, complete OAuth
- reconnect: Re-authenticate expired session
- none: Fully connected, no action needed

connected_by_display_name.description: Display name of user who connected

connected_by_email.description: Email of the user who connected

connected_by_user_id.description: Azure AD Object ID of user who connected

updated_at.description: When the connection was last updated

expires_at.description: When the current access token expires

is_token_valid.description: Whether the current token is still valid

SignatureSetting.description: Setting that allows to add a signature.

EmailDomainSetting.description: Setting that allows to add a custom domain. For e.g; doe.com

EmailAddressSetting.description: Setting that allows to add an email address on the custom domain. For e.g; john@doe.com

WhitelistEmailAddressSetting.description:
- Setting that specifies a list of addresses exempt from being flagged as duplicate emails.
- An email will be flagged as a duplicate if it has the same content and is sent to the same recipient within the time frame specified in the RestrictDuplicatesWithinSetting.

RestrictDuplicatesWithinSetting.description:
- Restrict duplicates within:
  * 10s
  * 5m
  * 1d
  * 5000 // It converts to 5 seconds.When expressed as a numerical value, it will be interpreted as being in milliseconds.
- Defaults to 3 minutes
- Negative values will be treated same as positive values
- If not set, defaults to 3 min
- If set as 0, then the no email will be treated as a duplicate
- Cannot have multiple values

Setting.description:
Generic setting object used for various email configuration types.
The applicable fields depend on the setting type:
- **signature**: Uses `name`, `value` (plain text), and `html` (rich text)
- **email_domain**: Uses `value` (domain name)
- **whitelist_email_address**: Uses `value` (email address)
- **restrict_duplicates_within**: Uses `value` (time duration)

name.description: Display name of the setting (used for signatures)

org_id.description: Organization ID that owns this setting

value.description:
The setting value. Interpretation depends on type:
- signature: Plain text version of the signature
- email_domain: Domain name (e.g., "yourcompany.com")
- whitelist_email_address: Email address to whitelist
- restrict_duplicates_within: Time duration (e.g., "5m", "1d")

html.description: HTML content (only applicable for signature type)

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the setting was created

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the setting was last updated

updated_by.description: User ID of the last editor

domain.description:
The domain name to add or verify. Can be a root domain or subdomain.
Examples: "yourcompany.com", "mail.yourcompany.com"
